items.prompt: Which property set do relational databases emphasize for transactional integrity?
choices: Only eventual consistency with no schema
items.explain: Section 12.2.2 states relational databases ensure integrity through ACID properties.

items.prompt: NoSQL databases are typically designed to scale horizontally across many machines.
items.explain: The chapter notes NoSQL systems distribute data across machines for horizontal scale.

items.prompt: According to Example 12.1, which workload is a typical NoSQL fit?
choices: High-ingest IoT streams with evolving schemas
Only offline CD-ROM archives with no writes
items.explain: Example 12.1 cites social/messaging apps, semi-structured logs, and high-ingest IoT streams.

items.prompt: Name one AWS object or database service paired in Example 12.2.
According to the SQL versus NoSQL comparison, NoSQL systems always require full ACID support for every write.
items.explain: Table 12.1 notes NoSQL often has limited or specialized transaction models and often eventual consistency.
